acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Development foundation and verification.
slices.intended_behavior: A developer can install Python and frontend dependencies, start API/frontend/worker services, run manifest preview, and run local compile, lint, Python test, frontend install, and frontend build checks.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-001: Review Development foundation and verification
risk_notes: Local checks do not exercise host Postgres/Redis services or an interactive browser smoke path.
Branch protection requirements may include remote checks outside the local release gate.
slices.title: Review Development foundation and verification
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Warmer-first product and data model.
slices.intended_behavior: Users compare the same final task across controlled variations in model, system prompt, artifact inputs, evaluators, and structured prior conversation context.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-002: Review Warmer-first product and data model
risk_notes: Context-sensitivity metrics can be over-interpreted if users ignore warnings about heuristic or uncalibrated signals.
Broad V2/V3 backlog pressure could dilute the warmer-first wedge.
slices.title: Review Warmer-first product and data model
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Manifest contract and run generation.
slices.intended_behavior: The same parser and expander validate manifests, report actionable errors, generate deterministic logical runs and attempts, and preserve normalized plus raw provider fields.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-003: Review Manifest contract and run generation
risk_notes: Manifest expansion is central to many surfaces; schema drift can break API, CLI, frontend builder, Promptfoo import/export, and suite reruns together.
Unsupported design types must continue to fail clearly rather than silently producing incomplete experiment matrices.
slices.title: Review Manifest contract and run generation
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Persistence, migrations, and immutable snapshots.
slices.intended_behavior: Historical experiments remain reproducible when library records change, retries/reruns do not overwrite logical runs, and persisted payloads redact sensitive provider or artifact metadata where required.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-004: Review Persistence, migrations, and immutable snapshots
risk_notes: Repository helpers are a large shared surface; small schema or snapshot changes can affect API, CLI, frontend, analytics, exports, and demos.
SQLite-oriented test paths may not catch every Postgres-specific migration or constraint behavior.
slices.title: Review Persistence, migrations, and immutable snapshots
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Project library and experiment API.
slices.intended_behavior: The frontend and external clients can manage Model Eval workflows through typed JSON boundaries while backend services enforce project scope and shared validation.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-005: Review Project library and experiment API
risk_notes: API response DTOs are manually mirrored by TypeScript types, so drift can create frontend runtime failures.
Many endpoints live in a single main.py module, increasing merge conflict and review risk for future API changes.
slices.title: Review Project library and experiment API
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Provider adapters, executor lifecycle, and queueing.
slices.intended_behavior: Runs advance through safe local-first execution; outbound calls require explicit policy clearance, and attempts preserve provider payloads, errors, timing, tokens, cost, retry, replicate, and cache metadata.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-006: Review Provider adapters, executor lifecycle, and queueing
risk_notes: Provider APIs and pricing metadata can drift outside the repo; adapters need periodic live-contract review before production use.
Queue wrappers are intentionally thin and testable without Redis, but real worker/runtime behavior still needs environment-level smoke coverage.
slices.title: Review Provider adapters, executor lifecycle, and queueing
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Artifact storage and preprocessing.
slices.intended_behavior: Runs record exactly what model inputs used, derived outputs remain metadata-first and local, unsafe paths or raw private payloads are excluded from API previews and export metadata, and missing derived inputs fail before provider execution.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-007: Review Artifact storage and preprocessing
risk_notes: PDF/image/OCR behavior depends on local PyMuPDF, Pillow, and optional OCR tooling.
Artifact paths and local storage URIs require strict redaction discipline across API, UI, exports, traces, and audit logs.
slices.title: Review Artifact storage and preprocessing
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Benchmark suites, dataset splits, and replicates.
slices.intended_behavior: Users can rerun locked suites over selected splits, preserve suite membership snapshots, and measure nondeterminism from replicate attempts without counting retry recovery as reliability evidence.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-008: Review Benchmark suites, dataset splits, and replicates
risk_notes: Suite reruns reuse manifest and execution machinery; regressions in one path can affect both suite and non-suite experiments.
Small replicate counts can produce visible but low-confidence intervals.
slices.title: Review Benchmark suites, dataset splits, and replicates
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Blind review, reviewer queues, and failure taxonomies.
slices.intended_behavior: Human reviewers can score output pairs without hidden metadata leakage, submit typed decisions, revise decisions, work through assigned queues, and use versioned failure taxonomy labels.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-009: Review Blind review, reviewer queues, and failure taxonomies
risk_notes: Metadata reveal and export paths must preserve blind review guarantees until explicit reveal.
Reviewer and taxonomy metadata are embedded in scores and exports, so schema changes need backward-compatible migration planning.
slices.title: Review Blind review, reviewer queues, and failure taxonomies
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Deterministic evaluators, LLM judges, and metric adapters.
slices.intended_behavior: Users can apply local deterministic checks, run calibrated judge workflows over stored attempts, and execute compatible local metric adapters while preserving typed scores and evaluator snapshots.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-010: Review Deterministic evaluators, LLM judges, and metric adapters
risk_notes: LLM judge outputs can look authoritative even when they are dry-run, uncalibrated, or weakly calibrated against human labels.
Local deterministic metrics and DeepEval-style fixture mapping may not represent live external SDK behavior.
slices.title: Review Deterministic evaluators, LLM judges, and metric adapters
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Results analytics, divergence, uncertainty, and frontier.
slices.intended_behavior: Results favor count-backed directional summaries, label low-confidence or heuristic metrics, filter consistently across API/UI/export paths, and avoid implying false precision.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-011: Review Results analytics, divergence, uncertainty, and frontier
risk_notes: Heuristic divergence and carryover rows can be mistaken for calibrated semantic judgments if warnings are hidden downstream.
Quality comparisons require comparable case/split groupings; analytics must keep filtering and grouping rules explicit.
slices.title: Review Results analytics, divergence, uncertainty, and frontier
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Frontend workbench.
slices.intended_behavior: Users can operate the core local workbench from the browser with typed API calls, client-side manifest building, monitor filters, blind review workflows, and analytics/export controls.
risk_notes: The frontend is concentrated in a large App.tsx file, raising review and conflict risk for future UI changes.
Manual TypeScript DTO mirroring can drift from backend schema changes.
slices.title: Review Frontend workbench
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Headless CLI and stable exports.
slices.intended_behavior: Users can run reproducible workflows without the UI, with stable export ordering, hidden metadata protection for blind review exports, and local-only defaults for execution commands.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-013: Review Headless CLI and stable exports
risk_notes: CLI commands depend on local database URL and PYTHONPATH/package install state; docs include fallback commands for checkout use.
Export formats are stable contracts and require compatibility care when score or analytics payloads change.
slices.title: Review Headless CLI and stable exports
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Promptfoo and OpenTelemetry interoperability.
slices.intended_behavior: Interop paths preserve raw provider params where possible, warn on unsupported or lossy Promptfoo mappings, and export trace metadata without private prompts, artifacts, warmer text, payloads, outputs, credentials, paths, checksums, or OCR text.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-014: Review Promptfoo and OpenTelemetry interoperability
risk_notes: Promptfoo schemas and provider formats can change externally; mapping logic needs compatibility review before relying on current behavior.
Trace export must remain allow-list based because it crosses many sensitive domain objects.
slices.title: Review Promptfoo and OpenTelemetry interoperability
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Privacy, reproducibility, and audit safety.
slices.intended_behavior: The system blocks unsafe outbound execution before provider calls, keeps provider credentials in environment variables, snapshots reproducibility metadata, and avoids leaking raw prompts, private artifacts, manifests, warmer text, request/response payloads, outputs, local paths, checksums, OCR text, or credentials into audit and trace metadata.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-015: Review Privacy, reproducibility, and audit safety
risk_notes: Live provider calls depend on environment credentials and explicit policy changes; accidental relaxation would increase egress risk.
Audit and trace redaction must stay synchronized with new model fields and export rows.
slices.title: Review Privacy, reproducibility, and audit safety
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.

acceptance_criteria: Review completed and any changes verified.
slices.description: Review and identify safe refactor opportunities for Copper memo demos and V2 readiness audit.
slices.intended_behavior: Developers can run MVP and V2 demo commands repeatedly, inspect outputs in the UI, and use generated reports as local artifacts while keeping generated export files out of git.
slices.pr_title: [codebase-review] SLICE-016: Review Copper memo demos and V2 readiness audit
risk_notes: Synthetic demo signals are useful for smoke coverage but should not be treated as real provider quality evidence.
Generated export directories should remain uncommitted unless a future task explicitly asks for checked-in sample output.
slices.title: Review Copper memo demos and V2 readiness audit
slices.why_this_slice_exists: Initial conservative slice from feature model.
